channel.title: AutoTrend Atlas Trending
channel.description: Autonomous trend radar for geopolitics, tech, macro and market-moving stories.

item.title: Anwar to Outline Malaysia Oil Supply Continuity Plan: Bernama
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will unveil a plan soon to shore up the country’s oil supply as uncertainties stemming from the conflict in Iran continue, Bernama reported.

item.title: Anwar to Outline Malaysia Oil Supply Continuity Plan: Bernama
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim will unveil a plan soon to shore up the country’s oil supply as uncertainties stemming from the conflict in Iran continue, Bernama reported.

item.title: ‘Missiles, drones locked on US targets’: Iran warns of ‘heavy assault’ after tanker strikes in Gulf of Oman
item.description: Iran's IRGC has warned of a "heavy assault" on US military assets and ships if Iranian oil tankers or commercial vessels are attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. This follows US forces disabling two Iranian oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman and an earlier exchange of fire near the strait.

item.title: Chalmers on 'Unacceptable' Australian Housing Status Quo
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the 2026-27 Federal Budget on May 12. It's framed as one the country's most consequential Budgets against a backdrop of inflation-fuelling global energy shocks, climbing cost-of-living, slowing productivity and a national debt edging toward A$1 trillion. Chalmers speaks

item.title: Chalmers on 'Unacceptable' Australian Housing Status Quo
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers will hand down the 2026-27 Federal Budget on May 12. It's framed as one the country's most consequential Budgets against a backdrop of inflation-fuelling global energy shocks, climbing cost-of-living, slowing productivity and a national debt edging toward A$1 trillion. Chalmers speaks with Bloomberg's James Mayger in Canberra. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Trellix source code breach claimed by RansomHouse hackers
item.description: The attack on the Trellix source code repository disclosed last week has been claimed by the RansomHouse threat group, which leaked a small set of images as proof of the intrusion. [...]

item.title: Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 26% as Iran War Drives Oil Prices
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported a 26% jump in first-quarter profit following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels, and as it redirected exports via a pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Aramco Profit Tops Estimates After War-Driven Oil Price Rise
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported first-quarter profit that surpassed analysts’ expectations following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels.

item.title: Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 26% as Iran War Drives Oil Prices
item.description: Saudi Aramco reported a 26% jump in first-quarter profit following a war-induced rise in prices of oil and refined fuels, and as it redirected exports via a pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. In 2026, hackers are using AI to make these "first clicks" nearly impossible to spot. If a single laptop get

item.title: Qatar Sends First LNG Shipment Through Hormuz Since War Started
item.description: A tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appears to have transited the Strait of Hormuz, marking the country’s first export out of the region since the Iran war began.

item.title: Qatar Sends First LNG Shipment Through Hormuz Since War Started
item.description: A tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appears to have transited the Strait of Hormuz, marking the country’s first export out of the region since the Iran war began.

item.title: Gustavo Petro Warns Fossil-Fueled Crypto Mining Will Trigger 'Climate Collapse' - Bitcoin News
item.description: Gustavo Petro Warns Fossil-Fueled Crypto Mining Will Trigger 'Climate Collapse' Bitcoin News

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Edge Plaintext Passwords, ICS 0-Days, Patch-or-Die Alerts and 25+ New Stories
item.description: Bad week. Turns out the easiest way to get hacked in 2026 is still the same old garbage: shady packages, fake apps, forgotten DNS junk, scam ads, and stolen logins getting dumped into Discord channels like it’s normal. Some of these attack chains don’t even feel sophisticated anymore. More like some tired guy with a Te

item.title: Canvas login portals hacked in mass ShinyHunters extortion campaign
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang has breached education technology giant Instructure again, this time exploiting another vulnerability to deface Canvas login portals for hundreds of colleges and universities. [...]

item.title: The Iran war will change global energy markets in these important ways, oil executives say
item.description: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has resulted in the loss of nearly a billion barrels of oil with the shortage growing worse every day.

item.title: The Iran war will change global energy markets in these important ways, oil executives say
item.description: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has resulted in the loss of nearly a billion barrels of oil with the shortage growing worse every day.

item.title: Whirlpool hikes prices again as Iran War, inflation shake appliance market - Scripps News
item.description: Whirlpool hikes prices again as Iran War, inflation shake appliance market Scripps News

item.title: Far-Right Australian Party Wins Seat in First Election Victory
item.description: A far-right party won its first lower house election in Australia, with the victory underscoring the collapse in support for the center-right coalition that’s governed for much of the post-war period.

item.title: Taiwan’s Plastic Habit Collides With Shortages Caused by a Faraway War - The New York Times
item.description: Taiwan’s Plastic Habit Collides With Shortages Caused by a Faraway War The New York Times

item.title: Dozens killed in jihadist attacks in central Mali
item.description: Jihadist fighters launched a fresh wave of deadly attacks in central Mali that killed dozens of people, local and security sources said on Saturday. One local official said the latest attacks brought the death toll to more than 70 in recent days, as Islamist armed groups intensify assaults on villages across the region.

item.title: Ransomware attack wipes out finals week at ISU, thousands of other schools - Idaho State Journal
item.description: Ransomware attack wipes out finals week at ISU, thousands of other schools Idaho State Journal

item.title: Ransomware attack wipes out finals week at ISU, thousands of other schools - Idaho State Journal
item.description: Ransomware attack wipes out finals week at ISU, thousands of other schools Idaho State Journal

item.title: Iran war live: IRGC warns US against attacks on ships; Israel bombs Lebanon
item.description: US-Iran ceasefire holds as Tehran warns Washington against attacks on tankers and Israel kills 24 people in Lebanon.

item.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
item.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
item.description: A previously undocumented Linux implant codenamed Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems to establish a silent foothold as well as facilitate a broad range of post-compromise functionality, such as credential harvesting, keylogging, file manipulation, clipboard monitoring, and network tunneling. "QLNX

item.title: Traders Looking for Next Leg in Global Stocks Rally Bet on Asia
item.description: As the focus moves away from the Iran war, investors and strategists alike are looking for the next leg up in equities. Many are turning to Asia.

item.title: Traders Looking for Next Leg in Global Stocks Rally Bet on Asia
item.description: As the focus moves away from the Iran war, investors and strategists alike are looking for the next leg up in equities. Many are turning to Asia.

item.title: Iran War Is Draining World's Oil Buffer at an Unprecedented Pace
item.description: submitted by /u/RRaj007 [link] [comments]

item.title: Zara data breach exposed personal information of 197,000 people
item.description: Hackers who gained access to the databases of Spanish fast-fashion retailer Zara stole data belonging to more than 197,000 customers, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]
